rule: Precompiled graph-node kernels must receive BUFFER-IDENTITY inputs unless the kernel itself supports base-buffer+offset ABI. Do NOT pass sliced/cache views across the precompiled-call boundary when whole-buffer offset math can be done inside the kernel.
mechanism: callify.transform_precompiled_call force-.contiguous() on every input EXCEPT Ops.AFTER/Ops.BIND; _precompiled_output_redirect returns a BUFFER/MULTI with has_buffer_identity() directly (no copy) but a SLICE/RESHAPE falls through to t.store(s) = MATERIALIZE. has_buffer_identity() walks RESHAPE/MULTI->BUFFER but the redirect only accepts op in {BUFFER,MULTI}, so a RESHAPE *on top of* the AFTER still materializes.
good: whole cache_kv.after(store) passed with NO reshape (kernel reads flat via base pointer)
separate whole K/V buffers if chosen
